title: onsemi Introduces Industry-First Elite Pairing Studio to Simplify Power Design
author: Na
description: onsemi launched the Elite Pairing Studio, an industry-first cloud-based design tool that helps engineers quickly identify optimal pairings of SiC MOSFETs and gate drivers for power electronics applications. The interactive simulation platform accelerates development by enabling early evaluation of device-level switching behavior and trade-offs, reducing design iterations for AI data centers, electric vehicles, and industrial systems.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: onsemi announced a significant innovation with the Elite Pairing Studio that addresses a real engineering challenge, potentially accelerating customer design cycles and reducing time-to-market. The tool expands their design ecosystem and positions them as a comprehensive solution provider, which should strengthen customer relationships and competitive positioning in growing markets like EVs and AI data centers.

title: AI-Chip Rally Powers S&P 500, Nasdaq 100, Dow To Fresh Records: Stock Market Today
author: Piero Cingari
description: U.S. stock indices reached fresh record highs on Tuesday, driven by a surge in semiconductor and AI-infrastructure stocks. Marvell Technology soared 20% after Nvidia's CEO praised it as 'the next trillion-dollar company' and committed a $2 billion investment. Hewlett Packard Enterprise surged 30% on strong earnings and AI orders. Optical networking and chip stocks rallied broadly, while software stocks pulled back significantly.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock surged 29.74% after Nvidia CEO called it 'the next trillion-dollar company' and Nvidia committed a $2 billion investment, signaling strong confidence in the company's AI data center prospects.
Stock gained 16.43% following strong fiscal Q2 results with 40% revenue growth, $1.8 billion in new AI systems orders, and raised full-year guidance.

Stock rose 13.37% benefiting from AI data-center connectivity theme and optical networking demand.
Stock gained 13.33% riding the AI data-center connectivity theme for optical components.
Stock rose 5.3% ahead of earnings announcement, benefiting from semiconductor and AI infrastructure rally.
Stock added more than 5% after SK Hynix flagged higher wafer capacity, supporting semiconductor demand.
Stock gained more than 5% following SK Hynix's higher wafer capacity announcement supporting chip demand.

title: 3 Stocks Rallying on Micron’s Price Boost: Substance or Hype?
author: Dan Schmidt
description: UBS analyst Timothy Arcuri raised Micron Technology's price target to $1625, citing a fundamental shift in high-bandwidth memory (HBM) from cyclical to AI-driven structural growth. This sparked a semiconductor rally, with three stocks gaining sympathy: Western Digital (strong fundamentals with long-term agreements), Rambus (undervalued IP licensing play), and onsemi (sympathy rally lacking substance, appearing overvalued).

insights.sentiment_reasoning: UBS raised price target by 200% to $1625, citing structural growth tailwinds from AI infrastructure demand, long-term supply agreements locked in through 2026, and concentrated supply chain with only three major HBM producers. Stock trading at attractive 10x forward earnings vs. NASDAQ 100 average of 24x.

Similar thesis to Micron applies with long-term hyperscaler agreements and claimed production capacity through 2026. Fiscal Q3 2026 earnings showed strong double-beat with 45% YOY revenue growth and 50%+ gross margins. Technical indicators show bullish momentum with support at 50-day moving average.

Classic 'picks and shovels' play on memory storage theme with high-margin IP licensing providing recurring revenue. Launched industry's fastest HBM4E Memory Controller in April. Stock up 60% YTD but remains undervalued relative to AI space peers. Technical setup shows bullish reversal with 50-day moving average as support.

Sympathy rally lacks substance as company's core business (automotive and industrial) is cyclical and not tied to AI. Data center revenue only $500M of projected $6B+ total revenue. Stock up 90% in three months appearing frothy, well above trend with MACD showing losing momentum. Recommended to take profits.
Entire semiconductor industry rallied 6% following Micron's price target boost, reflecting broad sector enthusiasm around AI-driven HBM demand and structural growth tailwinds.

title: Nasdaq 100 Hits Record Above 30,000 On Micron's 18% Rally: Stock Market Today
author: Piero Cingari
description: The Nasdaq 100 crossed 30,000 during intraday trading as semiconductor stocks surged, led by Micron Technology's 18% gain following a UBS analyst price target hike to $1,625. The rally was driven by increased AI spending expectations from hyperscalers, with chip stocks dominating market leadership. The S&P 500 rose 0.6%, while the Russell 2000 outperformed with a 1.7% gain. AutoZone plunged 10.1% after missing revenue estimates despite beating EPS.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock surged 18% on UBS analyst price target hike to $1,625 (from $535), driven by strong AI memory demand expectations. Market cap exceeded $1 trillion for the first time, with stock up 200% year-to-date.
Jumped 12.3% tracking the broader semiconductor rally and benefiting from announced cash dividend, with no company-specific negative catalyst.
Rose 9.1% on sympathy buying with the semiconductor sector rotation, driven by positive AI infrastructure spending sentiment.
Climbed 8.7% lifted by the chip sector rotation and increased AI-related spending expectations.
Added 8.4% as part of the broader semiconductor and tech sector rally driven by AI infrastructure demand.
Gained 6.8% as cheaper jet fuel and Iran deal narrative boosted airline stocks, lifting the broader airline sector.
Plunged 10.1% despite beating EPS estimates, due to revenue miss ($4.84B vs $4.88B estimate) and soft domestic same-store sales of 4.1% against elevated expectations.
Dropped 6% as investors trimmed positions ahead of May 28 earnings announcement.

title: Stock Market Today: Nasdaq 100 Rises Despite Hot PPI, Nvidia Hits Record High
author: Piero Cingari
description: Tech stocks surged on Wednesday despite a hotter-than-expected April PPI reading (6% YoY vs 4.8% expected), which rekindled inflation concerns. The Nasdaq 100 gained 0.7% led by semiconductor strength, while rate-sensitive sectors like utilities and financials declined. Nvidia hit record highs above $226 ahead of earnings, Ford surged 11.9% on strong Q1 results, and software stocks like Dynatrace and Doximity fell sharply on disappointing earnings.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock rallied for sixth consecutive session to above $226, market cap exceeded $5.5 trillion, and CEO Jensen Huang joining Trump's China delegation signals strategic importance and investor confidence ahead of earnings
Surged 11.9% after delivering Q1 revenue of $43.3B and adjusted EPS of $0.66 (both ahead of consensus) while raising 2026 EBIT guidance to $8.5-10.5B
